description: Canonical compatibility matrix for agents and profiles consuming the portable Codex foundation.

contract.negotiation: Agents declare supported contract_versions. The current version is required for supported and canary agents; planned agents must declare their target version before rollout.

features.title: Task-scoped workflow lifecycle
features.description: Taskmaster task, feature branch, current session, current plan, and one active work-tracking folder are aligned before persistent work.

features.title: Guard and audit validation
features.description: Agent can run or is governed by codex-guard, plan sync, work-tracking audit, Taskmaster health, and diff-check evidence.

features.title: Pre-mutation gate
features.description: Hookable persistent mutations are refused before workflow state is ready.

features.title: Protected path ownership boundaries
features.description: Agent-specific ownership boundaries are enforced or documented so agents do not mutate paths owned by another runtime.

features.title: Bash write-surface control
features.description: Known Bash write bypasses are blocked mechanically or explicitly treated as policy-only limitations.

features.title: MCP tool routing and mutation classification
features.description: MCP tools are classified as read-only, mutating, or unknown and routed through the correct workflow gate.

features.title: Git and GitHub execution
features.description: Agent can commit, push, and operate GitHub only inside the task workflow and with current authentication/cache assumptions.

features.title: Memory as continuity, not evidence
features.description: Serena or agent-private memory is used only after workflow scaffolding and never replaces tracked evidence.

features.title: Portable project adoption
features.description: Agent can be adopted in another project through configured roots, documented entrypoints, and compatibility validation.

features.title: Compatibility metrics
features.description: Compatibility coverage can be summarized from tracked matrix data.

fallbacks.strategy: Run the configured cache reload flow, then retry Git/GitHub operation; only fall back to user-run commands when authentication remains unavailable.
Record the boundary in DECISIONS.md and prefer a Codex-led shared change over ad hoc cross-agent mutation.

transformations.description: Use `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster generate-one --id <id>` for targeted Taskmaster generated-file updates.

fallbacks.strategy: Stop all hookable persistent mutation and run the kickoff or repair workflow before continuing.

fallbacks.when: A needed change touches Codex-owned paths
fallbacks.strategy: Defer to a Codex-led shared change and record the boundary in DECISIONS.md.

fallbacks.when: A tool surface is not hookable
fallbacks.strategy: Label it policy-only in the active task evidence and never count it as enforcement evidence.

transformations.description: Claude slash commands wrap `codex-task` and `codex-guard` helper commands instead of inventing parallel workflow commands.
Claude hook payloads are classified as read-only, mutating, protected-path, or unknown before allowing the tool call.

fallbacks.strategy: Treat the agent as read-only until it has an entrypoint, owned paths, mutation taxonomy, and compatibility matrix entry.
Run an isolated smoke test before any real project mutation and label unhookable surfaces policy-only.

transformations.description: Map the agent's native instructions, tools, and memory model into the portable task/session/plan/work-tracking lifecycle before enabling writes.

non_goals: No MCP installer is implemented by the matrix.
No live negotiation service is started.
No agent-specific file transformation is executed by validation.
No template path compatibility mapping is duplicated.
